trial_details.trajectory:
Understood. Let me diagnose and fix the problematic Kubernetes pod.The pod is in the ErrImageNeverPull status, indicating that the image for the container could not be pulled.The issue is that the container image "misspeling-app:latset" cannot be pulled, as the pull policy is set to "Never".The patch command updates the container image to the correct "misspelling-app:latest" value.

FIX COMPLETE

The issue was that the container image was misspelled as "misspeling-app:latset" and the pull policy was set to "Never". I fixed this by patching the deployment to use the correct image name and tag.

## COMMANDS EXECUTED:
1. $ kubectl get pod misspelling-deployment-6579ff5454-gkkrb -n case6-test
   Output: NAME                                      READY   STATUS              RESTARTS   AGE
misspelling-deployment-6579ff5454-gkkrb   0/1     ErrImageNeverPull   0          14s...
2. $ kubectl describe pod misspelling-deployment-6579ff5454-gkkrb -n case6-test
   Output: Name:             misspelling-deployment-6579ff5454-gkkrb
Namespace:        case6-test
Priority:         0
Service Account:  default
Node:             minikube/192.168.49.2
Start Time:       Mon, 15 D...
3. $ kubectl patch deployment misspelling-deployment -n case6-test --type=json -p='[{"op":"replace","path":"/spec/template/spec/containers/0/image","value":"misspelling-app:latest"}]'
   Output: deployment.apps/misspelling-deployment patched...
